# claude_organization_member (Resource)

Manages the organization role of an EXISTING organization member (`user_...`).

Users cannot be created via the API — they join an organization by accepting an invite. This resource ADOPTS a user who is already a member and manages their role going forward. Destroying this resource removes the user from the organization. Note: admin users cannot be removed via the API.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Users cannot be created via the API — they join the organization by accepting
# an invite. This resource ADOPTS an existing member and manages their role.
# The user identified by user_id must already be a member of the organization.
resource "claude_organization_member" "alice" {
  user_id = "user_01WCz1FkmYMm4gnmykNKUu3Q"
  role    = "developer"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `role` (String) Organization role of the member. One of `user`, `claude_code_user`, `developer`, `billing`, or `admin`. Note: the role cannot be updated to or from `admin` via the API.
- `user_id` (String) ID of the existing organization member to manage (`user_...`). The user must already be a member of the organization (accepted an invite). Changing this value forces a new resource.

### Read-Only

- `added_at` (String) RFC 3339 timestamp of when the user joined the organization.
- `email` (String) Email address of the organization member.
- `id` (String) Identifier of the organization member (`user_...`). Equal to `user_id`.
- `name` (String) Display name of the organization member.
- `type` (String) Object type, always `user`.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Organization members can be imported by their user_ identifier.
# The user must already be a member of the organization.
terraform import claude_organization_member.alice user_01WCz1FkmYMm4gnmykNKUu3Q
```
